status.text: %2% Plugin '%3%': Address '%1%' is already in use by the %4% plugin '%5%'.
status.description: A stack element plugin is attempting to use a network address that is already in use within another plugin.

status.text: Failed to connect to the StatEngine at location '%1%': %2%.
status.description: Aptixia failed to connect to an Stat Engine instance. Please ensure network connectivity to the indicated address.

status.text: Failed to obtain write lock for object '%1%:%2%'. Locked by '%3%'
status.description: Aptixia failed to obtain a write lock for the object.

status.text: The lengths of statSpecList and statFilterGroupList are mismatched
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. If the stat watch feature is used through the CORBA API, please make sure to specify a filter group list for every stat spec, even if the filter group is empty.

status.text: Could not connect to CORBA name service
status.description: Aptixia was unable to connect to the CORBA name service, omninames. Please ensure that omninames.exe is running.

status.text: Cannot register stat '%1%': %2%
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Illegal Operation On An Empty Container
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates that a delete operation was attempted on an empty list.

status.text: Bad index '%1%'
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates that an attempt was made to address a list element that does not exist.

status.text: Node Deserialization Error at line %1%, column %2%: %3%
status.description: Aptixia received an invalid XML configuration. If the failure happened on an Aptixia-generated XML string, this is an internal error.

status.text: %1%: Line %2%: Col %3%: Unexpected Element '%4%'
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: %1%: Line %2%: Col %3%: Unexpected RootNode '%4%'
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: XML Facility Is Not Initialized
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: XML File Error: File '%1%': %2%
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: XML Parser Error: Xml Line %1%: %2%: %3%
status.description: Internal Error: Aptixia received an invalid XML document, generated by the application.

status.text: Aptixia Internal Error - Please contact tech support
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Pop attempt on an empty list
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to pop an element from an empty list.

status.text: Property '%1%' does not exist
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates the use of a non-existing property in an xref operation.

status.text: %1% is not of type %2%
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates use of an operation on a property of a type not permitted for that operation.

status.text: Cannot Find Schema for '%1%'
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates attempting to use a class that is not registered with Aptixia.

status.text: Cannot Open Metaschema File '%1%'
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Session Property '%1%' is not available
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Wrong Type for XML Element '%1%':line %2%: column %3%: should be of type '%4%'
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates use of an operation on a property of a type not permitted for that operation.

status.text: Integer element '%1%' out of range: %2%. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set an integer to a value not permitted by Aptixia.

status.text: Integer element '%1%' is not numeric: %2%. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set an integer to an alphanumeric value.

status.text: Double element '%1%' out of range: %2%. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set a double float to a value not permitted by Aptixia.

status.text: Validation error for object %1% (id=%2%) : %3%.
status.description: The value provided for the indicated property is invalid.

status.text: Double element '%1%' is not numeric: %2%. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set a double float to an alphanumeric value.

status.text: String length of element '%1%' out of range: %2%. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set a string to a length not permitted by Aptixia.

status.text: Validation of element '%1%' with value '%2%' failed. %3%
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to set a string in a format not permitted by Aptixia.

status.text: Requested list length %2% for '%1%' out of range. Valid range is %3% to %4%.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates an attempt to operate on a list in a way that would result in the list having a length not permitted by Aptixia.

status.text: Deserialization error at row %1%, column %2%: Type %3% has no property '%4%'.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. When using the CORBA API, this indicates that an XML document contained a property Aptixia is unaware of. This is likely caused by loading an XML document from a inconsistent version of Aptixia.

status.text: Unable to enumerate message path '%1%'.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Unable to find stat '%1%' in resolved stat list.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. If generated when using the CORBA API, this indicates that an unavailable stat was specified in a StatView or a StatWatch.

status.text: Failed to register stat.
status.description: If generated by an application, this is an internal error. If generated when using the CORBA API, this indicates that an available stat was specified in a StatView or a StatWatch.

status.text: File '%1%' does not have the correct format: %2%.
status.description: The indicated file does not have the proper format.

status.text: Directory '%1%' Does not Exist
status.description: The indicated directory does not exist.

status.text: File '%1%' Does not Exist
status.description: The indicated file does not exist.

status.text: Cannot Find Resource '%1%' (%2%)
status.description: Cannot find the requested resource.

status.text: General Assertion Error
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: General Verification Error: %1%
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: System Exception Caught. Exception Text: %1%
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Error reading file %1% (%2%)
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Error writing file %1% (%2%)
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Error occured receiving internal data from an ixia port CPU: %1%
status.description: A communication error occurred between the Aptixia test server and an Ixia port CPU. Many times this is due to some external system error condition present on the Ixia port CPU. Please try the operation again after rebooting the port CPU.

status.text: The format of the port identifier %1% is incorrect
status.description: The client provided a port ID in an incorrect format. This is a internal error in the client. Please contact technical support.

status.text: The port corresponding to the identifier %1% is not available in the chassis
status.description: The client provided a port ID that does not correspond to a port on the connected chassis. This is a internal error in the client. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Cannot connect to Ixia Hardware Manager on the chassis %1%.
status.description: Please check to make sure that the chassis is up, IxServer is running and Ixia Hardware Manager service is running.

status.text: Cannot communicate with the chassis %1%.
status.description: Please check to make sure that the chassis is up, IxServer is running and Ixia Hardware Manager service is running.

status.text: Error in Corba Communication.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Error adding Chassis %1%.
status.description: The chassis could not be added to the chain due to a possible timeout. Please try again.

status.text: The Chassis %1% to be added is already in the chain
status.description: The chassis cannot be added again.

status.text: The card %1% does not exist in the chassis %2%.
status.description: The client is attempting to get information about a card that does not exist on the chassis.

status.text: The port %1% from card %2% in the chassis %3% does not exist.
status.description: The client is attempting to get information about a port that does not exist on the chassis.

status.text: Cannot take ownership of one or more port(s) in the chassis %1%.
status.description: The ports are owned by some other user, or the port did not respond.

status.text: Cannot clear ownership of the specifed port(s) in the chassis %1%.
status.description: The ports are owned by some other user, or the port did not respond.

status.text: Cannot reboot the port CPU on port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3%.
status.description: The port could not be rebooted because the port does not support this feature or it is owned by some other user.

status.text: Cannot set the port CPU on port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3% to factory defaults.
status.description: The port could not be reset to factory defaults because the port does not support this feature.

status.text: Cannot set the port changes to the port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3%.
status.description: The port is being used by another user or the configured parameters are incorrect for the port.

status.text: Cannot write the port changes to the port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3%.
status.description: The port is being used by another user, or the configured parameters are incorrect for the port.

status.text: Cannot write the Tcl portgroup changes in the chassis %1%.
status.description: Changes could not be written to the Tcl portgroup. Please check that the chassis is up and make sure none of the ports in the portgroup are owned by another user.

status.text: XML file for Layer1 Configuration could not be parsed in the chassis %1%.
status.description: The XML file is not well formed and cannot be parsed by the parser. This indicates a problem in the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: XML file sent to chassis %1% for Layer1 Configuration does not contain data.
status.description: The XML file does not contain data. This indicates a problem in the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: The XML file sent to chassis %1% for Layer1 Configuration does not have 'Aptixia' as root node.
status.description: The XML file was not constructed correctly by the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Cannot set the Layer1 parameters in the the port CPU of port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3%.
status.description: The port is being used by another user, or the configured parameters are incorrect for the port.

status.text: Error writing layer1 information using the portgroup.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: No method was specified in object-request.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: DOD (download on demand) upload to chassis %1% was attempted while the DOD object was in the middle of another upload.
status.description: This is a internal error on the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: DOD (download on demand) file segment upload to chassis %1% was attempted while the DOD object was in the middle of another upload.
status.description: This is a internal error on the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Could not set the base address %1% in the chassis.
status.description: Either the chassis IP address is incorrect or the message could not be sent to IxServer.

status.text: Could not clear the time stamp in the chassis %1%.
status.description: Either the chassis IP address is incorrect or the message could not be sent to IxServer.

status.text: Could not login to the chassis %1%
status.description: The login name was null or invalid. This is an internal error in the test server. Please contact technical support.

status.text: There was a mismatch in the IxOS versions between the master and the chassis %1% added to the chain.
status.description: A chassis added to a chain must have the same IxOS version as the master chassis.

status.text: Download of package(s) %1% failed on one or more ports belonging to chassis %2%
status.description: Either the portgroup is empty or the download failed in one or more ports. There might be a problem in the package, or the port may not be in the Ready state.

status.text: Download of package(s) %1% failed on one or more ports belonging to chassis %2%
status.description: Either the portgroup is empty or the delete failed in one or more ports.

status.text: Could not open file %1% on the chassis %2%.
status.description: The file was not found, or the user does not have permission to read the file.

status.text: Could not copy to file %1% on the chassis %2%.
status.description: Check that there is sufficient hard disk space on the chassis.

status.text: The DOD file %1% was not found on the testserver.
status.description: The file was not found, or the user does not have permission to read the file.

status.text: The DOD file %1% was not found on the testserver.
status.description: The file was not found, or the user does not have permission to read the file.

status.text: Could not copy DOD package to the temporary file %1% on the testserver.
status.description: Check that there is sufficient hard disk space on the test server.

status.text: Error interacting with InterfaceManager on the port CPU at '%1%': %2% . Could not connect to Ixia port CPU, please check the state of the port.
status.description: Could not connect to Ixia port CPU, please check the state of the port.

status.text: The command %1% is not valid.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: The object address given is invalid.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: The object id '%1%' does not resolve to an object. Method '%2%'.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: No matching method for '%1%' was found.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Parameter %1% was invalid.
status.description: This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Database Error '%1%'
status.description: Invalid Database Operation or Database is unavailable. This usually occurs if the database installation is corrupted or the database is unavailable. Please try the operation again after some time. If the problem persists, please contact technical support with the complete description of this error message.

status.text: Invalid StatConsumer (%1%)"
status.description: StatConsumer has not been initialized or StatConsumer has been not been associated with this Test. This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Invalid StatSetList (%1%)"
status.description: DataStore cannot process a null StatSetList. This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Invalid IIDList %1%
status.description: StatConsumer must initialize its IIDList before writing data to the DataStore. This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Invalid StatSpecList %1%
status.description: DataStore cannot register StatConsumer with a null StatSpecList. This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Invalid StatFilterGroupList %1%
status.description: DataStore cannot register StatConsumer with a null StatFilterGroupList. This is an internal error. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Invalid Operation ( %1%) on an archived TestResult
status.description: This operation cannot be performed on an archived TestResult. Please choose a valid operation or another TestResult.

status.text: Command cancellation response
status.description: Internal use only.

status.text: Error in IpToPortMapper %1%
status.description: Internal use only.

status.text: Test contains 1 or more invalid ports, or ports not belonging to connected chassis. Related port info is: %1%
status.description: If this occurred while doing a live configuration or while importing an unedited test XML file, this is an internal error. If this occurred while importing an XML file after manually editing the XML file, it could indicate an error in the XML file.

status.text: No ports have been assigned to the PortGroup %1%.
status.description: Please assign ports to the portgroup.

status.text: Link state is down for the following ports %1%.
status.description: The configuration fails if the link state is down for any port in the port group.

status.text: Link state is down for one or more ports
status.description: The configuration fails if the link state is down for any port in the port group.

status.text: IP address %1% is being used by port groups %2% and %3%
status.description: The configuration of duplicate IPs is not allowed.

status.text: MAC address %1% is being used by port groups %2% and %3%
status.description: The configuration of duplicate MACs is not allowed.

status.text: MAC address %1% is being used multiple times by port group %2%
status.description: The configuration of duplicate MACs is not allowed.

status.text: There are at least two %1% ranges with name: '%2%'. Please make sure every range has a unique name.
status.description: A plugin cannot have two ranges with the same name.

status.text: For %1% the range count is %2%. Only %3% addresses are allowed for %4% ranges with a subnet prefix of %5%
status.description: The number of IP interfaces to be created cannot be greater than what is allowed based on the prefix.

status.text: For %1% of type %2%, does not match the ip type %3% for Emulated Router.
status.description: The IP type for all IP ranges in the IPPlugin should match the IP type of the Emulated Router below it.

status.text: Value %1% not a legal %2% address. %3%.
status.description: Not a valid IP address.

status.text: Value %1% not a legal %2% address to increment by
status.description: Not a valid Increment By value.

status.text: Value %1% not a legal %2% gateway address. %3%.
status.description: Not a valid gateway address.

status.text: Value %1% not a legal IP type. Allowed types are %2%
status.description: Not a valid IP type.

status.text: Port %1% is not present in current chassis info
status.description: An invalid port is present in the port group.

status.text: IxServer process is running but not yet ready on the Chassis %1%
status.description: Please wait until IxServer reaches the ready state and then restart the test.

status.text: IxServer process is not running on the Chassis %1%
status.description: Please restart IxServer and then restart the test.

status.text: Timeout expired when waiting for all links to come up
status.description: Some of the ports in the portgroup do not have the link up.

status.text: Port %1% has a invalid layer1 type of %2%.
status.description: An invalid port is present in the port group.

status.text: Cannot set the port CPU of port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3% to factory defaults.
status.description: The port could not be reset to factory defaults because the port does not support this feature.

status.text: Cannot set the port CPU of port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3% to factory defaults.
status.description: The port could not be reset to factory defaults because an error occurred while writing the factory defaults to the IxServer.

status.text: Cannot set the port CPU of port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3% to mode defaults.
status.description: The port could not be reset to mode defaults because the port does not support this feature.

status.text: Cannot set the port CPU of port %1% in the card %2% belonging to chassis %3% to mode defaults.
status.description: The port could not be reset to mode defaults because an error occurred while writing the mode defaults to the IxServer.

status.text: Cannot clear statistics in one or more ports belonging to the chassis %1%.
status.description: There was a problem in clearing all statistics counters on one or more ports.

status.text: Cannot upload the translator module XML stream to the chassis %1%.
status.description: This is either because there was an invalid call sequence between the Ixia Test Server and the Hardware Manager, or not enough memory available on the chassis.

status.text: Could not start transmitting on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while starting packet transmit. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for starting transmission.

status.text: Could not stop the transmission on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while stopping packet transmit. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for stopping transmission.

status.text: Could not start the latency on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while starting the latency operation. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for starting the latency operation.

status.text: Could not stop the latency on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while stopping the latency operation. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for stopping the latency operation.

status.text: The specified transmit mode is not supported in port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%
status.description: The transmit mode is not supported for the specified port. Please specify the correct transmit mode.

status.text: The specified receive mode is not supported in port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%
status.description: The receive mode is not supported for the specified port. Please specify the correct receive mode.

status.text: The port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3% is owned by another user, so the specified operation cannot be performed.
status.description: Please use a different port or clear the ownership on this port before specifying the operation.

status.text: Could not obtain the port memory information from port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: An error occurred while attempting to obtain the port memory. Please check the port state and make sure it is in the Ready state.

status.text: Could not obtain the capture information from port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: An error occurred while attempting to obtain the port capture information. Please check the port state and make sure it is in the correct state.

status.text: Could not obtain the capture buffer information from port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: An error occurred while attempting to obtain the port capture buffer information. Please check the port state and make sure it is in the correct state.

status.text: No capture packets were received by the port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: Make sure the port is in the capture state and is connected to another port that is transmitting packets.

status.text: Could not export the capture buffer from the port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: An unknown error occurred while attempting to export the capture buffer from the port. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Could not import the port file to port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3% because the feature specifed in the file is not supported.
status.description: The import file information is not compatible with the type of port used.

status.text: Could not simulate cable connect/disconnect in the port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3% because the feature specifed in the file is not supported.
status.description: The cable connect/disconnect feature is not supported on this port.

status.text: An unknown error occurred while attempting an operation in the chassis %1%
status.description: Please contact technical support.

status.text: You do not have a license to use '%1%' plug-in.
status.description: The current application does not have a license to use the specified plug-in.

status.text: An error occurred while attempting to add the port %1% of card %1% in the chassis %3% to hwmanager's TclPortGroup.
status.description: Please contact technical support.

status.text: The chassis chain has been configured with an invalid value of cable length for the chassis %1%. The cable length must be one of 0.0, 3.0, 6.0, 9.0, 12.0, 15.0, 18.0, 21.0, 24.0
status.description: Please contact technical support.

status.text: Cannot download the translator module response stream from the chassis %1%.
status.description: This is either because there was a wrong call sequence between the Ixia Test Server and the Hardware Manager, or there was a memory problem in the chassis.

status.text: The chassis %1% is configured as master in the chassis chain but the chassis is actually a slave.
status.description: Information from the chassis shows that the chassis is really a slave chassis. Please reconfigure.

status.text: The chassis %1% is configured as slave in the chassis chain but the chassis is actually a master.
status.description: Information from the chassis shows that the chassis is really a master chassis. Please reconfigure.

status.text: One or more ports selected for reboot in the chassis %1% could not complete the reboot.
status.description: This indicates a problem in the chassis or IxServer.

status.text: The chassis %1% has more than one physical ethernet interface enabled. Hardware manager cannot work if more than one physical interface is enabled on the chassis. Please disable all but one physical ethernet interface on the chassis.
status.description: Currently , the Hardware Manager cannot connect to a chassis having more than one Ethernet card enabled.

status.text: The chassis %1% has been configured to have master as %2%. The master chassis has not been added by the client yet or is not valid.
status.description: Please add the master chassis before configuring the chassis chain.

status.text: An internal exception occurred while trying to import the specified port file to port %1% of card %2% in the chassis %3%.
status.description: The port file is either corrupted or of the wrong format. Please use the correct port file for import.

status.text: Could not start the capture on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while starting packet capture. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for start capture.

status.text: Could not stop the capture on one or more of the specified ports in the chassis %1%
status.description: There was a problem while stopping the packet capture. Please make sure the ports are in the correct state for stop capture.

status.text: Trying to get the chassis operation \"%1%\" failed for chassis %2%
status.description: One of the chassis operation failed on the chassis. This is a internal error in the client. Please contact technical support.

status.text: Cannot add default route to the gateway address %1%. %2%.
status.description: There was a problem while adding a default route to the configured gateway.

status.text: Failed to get a response from %1%
status.description: The port cpu component on the chassis failed to respond. Please check the IxServer log on the chassis
